title: Frequently Asked Questions
ruling: This section provides answers to a number of common questions that are asked about the game. The entries are presented in a question and answer format, with the newest questions at the end of the list.

question: Can [Daniela Reyes](/card/12001) use her [reaction] ability to trigger the [action][action] ability on [Sledgehammer](/card/12028)
ruling: When resolving Daniela's [reaction] ability, she is considered to immediately gain and then spend a single action on either a basic fight action or an eligible Fight ability on a card, even if it is not currently her turn. In order to pay the additional action cost of Sledgehammer's [action][action] ability, she must therefore have an additional action currently available to spend. This means that Daniela's [reaction] ability can be used to trigger [action][action] abilities, but only during her turn and only if she spends the second action required from her pool for the turn; at all other times, she is limited to abilities with a single action cost.

question: Can the [reaction] abilities on [Daniela Reyes](/card/12001) or [Service Revolver](/card/12008) target an enemy that attacked from a different location, or which has moved to a different location after attacking as a result of the elusive keyword?
ruling: Yes. Both of the above [reaction] abilities specifically target the enemy which performed the triggering attack, overriding the normal targeting rules of a fight action and allowing them to attack even an enemy that is presently at a different location.

question: What cards can [Miguel de la Cruz](/card/60551) use to help fight or evade his signature weakness, the [Feline Hybrid](/card/60553)?
ruling: The Feline Hybrid's immunity to player card effects means that it cannot be directly affected or targeted by an ability on a player card. For example, Miguel could not attack the Feline Hybrid with the [action] ability on [Pocketknife](/card/60559), as the resulting Fight action would attempt to target the Hybrid. Similarly, while he can trigger [Makeshift Bomb](/card/60581) in response to the Hybrid entering its location, it would deal no damage to the Hybrid as the player card effect itself is the one attempting to place that damage. However, if Miguel were to attempt a basic fight action against the Hybrid, a card like Vicious Blow could still be committed to his test and resolve its full effects, as Vicious Blow's +1 damage targets and modifies the basic fight action Miguel is performing, not the Hybrid.

question: Can [Marie Lambeau](/card/60451) heal or be healed by another investigator while [Called to Guinée](/card/60453)?
ruling: While Called to Guinée is in Marie's threat area, no ability or effect she controls can heal damage from any card. However, this does not prevent another investigator, or a scenario effect, from healing her or a card she controls.

question: Can Guerrilla Tactics be used to fight or evade an unengaged enemy with the aloof keyword?
ruling: Yes, but only if that enemy is at a connecting location. Guerrilla Tactics's ability has the option to resolve "as if" you are engaged with an enemy at a connecting location. If that enemy is aloof, this effect causes the play restriction applied by aloof to be fulfilled. However, if you do not choose to target an enemy at a connecting location, you also do not apply the "as if" effect and therefore cannot target an aloof enemy at your location with either test.
